query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve (EIA series WCSSTUS1):

**Current level:** 325.7 million barrels (325,655 thousand bbl) as of the week ending **June 26, 2026** — the lowest since May 1983.

**Trajectory over the past ~6 months:**
- **Dec 2025–mid-March 2026:** Flat plateau/slow fill, holding around 411.9–415.4 million barrels.
- **Late March–June 2026:** A sharp, sustained drawdown of roughly **89.8 million barrels in ~14 weeks**, with weekly draws frequently in the **8–10 million barrel** range from May through mid-June.

**Context:** The decline is attributed to a U.S. initiative to **release 172 million barrels** to offset global shortages following the Iran war and to lower fuel prices, amplified by high crude exports and near-record (~96%) refinery utilization. Total U.S. crude inventories (commercial + SPR) fell to ~743.3 million barrels as of June 19, 2026 — the lowest since 1984.

Data was sourced from EIA (WCSSTUS1) and corroborated by YCharts and Trading Economics. Note: the FRED tools returned no matches for this series, so I sourced directly from EIA and mirroring aggregators.
